title: How much of a cyborg an Evangelion really is
display_title: How much of a cyborg an Evangelion really is
wikitext:
[]: How much of a cyborg an Evangelion really is?             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Wed Jun 18, 2014 12:26 pm One thing is clear: EVAs aren't mecha in the traditional sense. They are humanoid weapons that are controlled from pilots on the inside, but that's pretty much as far as it goes, since they're mainly organic living creatures. But what about the cyborg elements? If they aren't actually piloted but controlled mentally by pilots who synchronize through the link provided by a human soul that is inside the EVA, there's no real need here for complicated mechanical parts. True, the pilots are controlling the EVA from plugs inserted into the neck, but that could be just because the connection with the soul is stronger there. The use of electrical signals to control the nerves and thus the organism's body doesn't seem to be present. Or is there a interplay between the two methods of control? Than again, there's the dummy system. How does the dummy system controls the EVA? Is this control mode more mechanical in nature. I've read on the forum about the dummy system tricking the EVA, but that doesn't seem to be enough. The pilots don't just trick the EVA, they control it. There needs to be a kind of way the dummy system controls the EVA, which may give me a reason to the question of how much is the EVA mechanical/electrical -"cyb"- in nature. Finally, the EVAs need an electrical plug to function. So, it needs electricity to function. Does this means that the mechanical component is crucial to it? Or it may have to do with the core? The Angels live because the S2 Engine gives them the vital energy, and several people agree that the S2 Engine is in the core, yet the EVAs are lacking a S2 in their normal configuration. As such, the electricity may be needed to give energy to the core, the same way a S2 Engine would do. The EVA has several obvious element inside that require electricity, but which seem to be important for the pilot (such as video displays), not as much to the EVA functioning. To summarize my thoughts into the most important questions: 1.The pilots control the EVA purely through a connection with the soul or need something else to make the connection work, like electrical impulses to the nervous system? If so, could someone better explain how this system fully works? 2.What is most of the electricity provided by the cable used for?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jun 19, 2014 5:15 am Funny my friend almost asked the same question lately. I think we have to divide between TNE and NGE canon. In TNE I'm not that sure, but in NGE it is: Evangelions are lving beings being constrained by technology. That technology is also used to help fusing the souls between the "mothers" and the children. But the most important part is the constraining. As you can see, the EVA's always running berserk, when the energy system fails to move and the life of pilot and Eva is in real danger. 1) No, because I doubt, anybody on the staff had a Phd in neuroscience, and scientific accuracy was always of least concern for NGE. 2)Constraining and emulating synapses between pilot and mech.

[]:             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:08 am Thanks Kaworu. Yeah, I was asking more than it could be answered with question 1. I knew that Ritsuko said in ep.20 that the EVAs are constrained, but it wasn't clear for me in what way. I was reading earlier more about the sync, and indeed there were clues from the series that NERV technology is needed so the whole sync process can work, like for example the system needing to operate in a certain language choose beforehand, thus maternal bond+software (ep. 1 and 8). So it seems that indeed they are true cyborgs, just not the kind of cyborgs where the technology is used to make the organism stronger or replace parts.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:20 am Thanks Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Thu Jun 19, 2014 8:25 am robersora wrote: No prob, Shinji-kun... Now, if you'd like to sleep by my side...

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Jun 19, 2014 8:50 am Just going off the decaying skeletal prototypes we've seen here and there, I'm guessing the mechanical parts of the Eva are the spine, some parts of the skull and maybe the arms. The entry plug sits in or near the cervical vertebrae, so it's likely that the absence of the plug or the signal termination plug used on Unit 00 is intended to cut off any electrical activity going from the brain to the body, leaving the Eva more or less quadriplegic. The mechanics on the neck probably link directly to the Eva's central nervous system, but we also see either in Rebuild or one of the supplements that the entry plug actually goes inside the Eva's core through a hole drilled in the back side. Like HiImAnAlien speculated, it seems to be a combination of the two methods that makes Eva piloting possible. The pilot gains control by going inside the Eva's core where the soul resides, then the mechanical parts give them enough control over the body to actually move it. The umbilical cable seems to provide power directly to the mechanical parts, but since the Evas have no S2 organ of their own, it's not unreasonable to guess that it probably powers the nervous system too.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jun 19, 2014 10:47 am HiImAnAlien wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Thu Jun 19, 2014 11:59 am BlueBasilisk wrote: The entry plug sits in or near the cervical vertebrae, so it's likely that the absence of the plug or the signal termination plug used on Unit 00 is intended to cut off any electrical activity going from the brain to the body, leaving the Eva more or less quadriplegic.
